title: Under-Bench Trundle Drawers
summary: Rolling drawers that tuck under a mudroom bench — no slides, just casters, so they pull straight out and hold boots, hats, and gear.
description:
The open space under a mudroom bench is prime storage, and this is the simplest way to claim it: casters instead of drawer slides. A shallow box on wheels rolls straight out from under the bench, which means no fussy slide alignment and no weight limit beyond what the casters carry. It's a great beginner build and endlessly adaptable to whatever bench you already have.

Everything is custom to your bench, so measure the opening first and plan for one or two drawers — the source specifically recommends avoiding a middle drawer, because with one or two drawers each can follow the bench legs and roll cleanly, while a middle drawer has nothing to track against. Build the box from 1x8s (or 1x6 or plywood), add a plywood bottom, and for heavier loads step the bottom up to 1/2" or 3/4" and consider a fifth center caster for support.

The drawer face is what makes it look built-in rather than like a bin shoved under a bench: build it from 1x3 and 1/2" plywood to overhang the box, and attach it after the drawer rolls right, with a small gap so it clears the bench frame. Add handles and finish to match the bench.
timeLabel: 3–5 hrs for one or two drawers

tools.note: Cleanest way to build the drawer box and face; countersunk screws or nails also work.

materials.name: 1x8 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Drawer box. 1x6 or 3/4" plywood also works.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood
materials.note: Drawer bottom; 1/2" or 3/4" for heavy loads.

materials.name: Caster wheels, 1"
materials.note: Add a 5th center caster for especially heavy loads.

cutList.note: Depth = your bench depth; example shown.
Width to fit the bench opening, minus clearance.
Sized to overhang the box and cover the opening.

steps.title: Plan the drawers
steps.body: Measure the space under the bench and decide on one or two drawers — avoid a middle drawer, so each drawer can follow the bench legs and roll straight. Figure your drawer size from the opening, leaving clearance to roll.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build a box from 1x boards — pocket holes, countersunk screws, or nails and glue all work. Keep it square so it rolls true.

steps.title: Add the bottom
steps.body: Add the plywood bottom with nails and glue. For larger drawers step up to 1/2" or 3/4" plywood, and for especially heavy loads plan on a fifth center caster.

steps.title: Add casters, face, and handles
steps.body: Screw casters to the base of the drawer and roll it under the bench to check the fit. Build the 1x3-and-1/2"-plywood face to overhang the box, attach it once the drawer rolls right (leaving a small gap so it clears the bench frame), and add handles. Finish to match the bench.

images.alt: Rolling storage drawers tucked under a mudroom bench
